title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The invention of the movable-type printing press by Johannes Gutenberg (0) in the mid-fifteenth century was a catalyst for the modern world. (9) Although books had existed for centuries, they were previously handwritten and extremely expensive for the average person to own. (10) Such was the efficiency of the new press that it allowed for the mass production of texts at an affordable cost. (11) Provided that a person could read, they suddenly had access to a vast world of knowledge and information. (12) Not only did the printing press facilitate the spread of religious ideas, but it also laid the foundation for the Scientific Revolution. (13) Regardless of the initial opposition from some authorities, the demand for printed material grew exponentially across the European continent. (14) Not until information could be shared rapidly through books did the standard of literacy begin to rise significantly. (15) Despite the digital age we live in now, the legacy of the printed word remains a cornerstone of our intellectual heritage. (16) Whether or not Gutenberg realized the scale of the change he had started is a question that historians often explore. This technological breakthrough remains one of the most significant events in human history.

example.text: The invention of the movable-type printing press (0) in the mid-fifteenth century.
example.explanation: Preposition used to indicate a point in time.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast.
Determiner used for emphasis before a noun phrase.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' correlative conjunction.
Part of the fixed linker 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('did the standard... begin').
Preposition used to introduce a contrast.
Conjunction introducing two possibilities or a doubt.
